markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: List feature/primitive/interaction registry rows that WP-009 should add during enrichment. This MT belongs to MasterSpecEnrichmentSeed. Prepare the future Master Spec refinement/enrichment payload without relying on sidecar research.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Prepare spec-enrichment-ready wording without editing the Master Spec in this stub task.
Where current Master Spec text is stale, mark the exact stale contract and the replacement Handshake-native rule.
scope.expected_behavior: AppendixFeatureRegistrySeed leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: AppendixFeatureRegistrySeed is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: AC1 (AppendixFeatureRegistrySeed specified for no-context continuation) -> the registry rows were not only listed but applied in .GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.192/spec-modules/12-end-of-file-appendices.md#12.7 WP-KERNEL-009 Primitive and Interaction Addendum: thirteen primitive rows (PRIM-ProjectKnowledgeIndex, PRIM-KnowledgeSource, PRIM-KnowledgeSpan, PRIM-KnowledgeEntity, PRIM-KnowledgeEdge, PRIM-KnowledgeClaim, PRIM-MemoryPassage, PRIM-RetrievalTrace, PRIM-RichDocument, PRIM-EditorCodeNode, PRIM-UserManual, PRIM-BackendNavigationReceipt, PRIM-VisualDebugEvidence) plus four interaction additions and the HBR applicability row (five 12.7 interaction/HBR bullets total, the fifth being the HBR row, not a sixth), with the addendum explicitly registering rows until the next full appendix generator pass folds them into the machine appendix blocks; the row plan is recorded in .GOV/task_packets/WP-KERNEL-009-Project-Knowledge-Index-Loom-Rich-Editor-v1/spec-enrichment-proposal.json module_change_plan for 12-end-of-file-appendices.md

AC2 (exact files, state tables, constraints, failure modes, verification evidence) -> exact files pinned in this MT scope.relevant_files and scope.constraints; registry consistency proven by refinement.json refinement.verification_results just spec-eof-appendices-check PASS and spec-bundle-check PASS (spec-eof-appendices and spec-growth-discipline checks); failure modes: spec-enrichment-proposal.json stale_conflict_examples for module 12 (appendices must resolve new primitive IDs)

AC3 (sidecar research rejected, ideas restated Handshake-native) -> stub research_basis.normative_status=non_normative_context_only + translation_rule; registry rows are Handshake-native primitives, not external references; this MT scope.constraints bar sidecar-paper and outside-runtime proof

AC4 (HBR rows named with concrete reasons) -> HBR-INT-001, HBR-MAN-001, HBR-SWARM-001 = NOT_APPLICABLE for runtime proof on this spec-seed record MT (no product surface, code, test, or runtime state changed by it; requirement content recorded for implementation MTs in 12-end-of-file-appendices.md#12.7, 10-product-surfaces.md#10.15.8 and #10.20); CX-503R PostgreSQL-only storage law = SATISFIED at spec level by 02-system-architecture.md#2.3.13.11 and 11-shared-dev-platform-and-oss-foundations.md#9.1.1 which lock PostgreSQL/EventLedger-only authority and bar SQLite for WP-KERNEL-009 paths; per packet.json acceptance_matrix.note HBR proof is carried per-MT

concerns: Contract freshness skew: scope.relevant_files and the activation constraint pin the v02.191 bundle while SPEC_CURRENT and all evidence are v02.192 (contracts hydrated 07:20Z after the 06:45Z spec bump); validator should read the constraint intent (indexed bundle, not v02.182 monolith), but a hygiene pass updating contract pins to v02.192 would remove the ambiguity.

Numeric overclaim: evidence reads "five interaction additions and the HBR applicability row", but 12.7 has exactly five interaction bullets TOTAL of which the fifth IS the HBR applicability row (four interaction edges + one HBR row). The thirteen primitive rows are correct. Fix: restate as four interaction additions plus the HBR row.
adversarial_review.refutation_attempts: Opened every cited artifact (refinement.json, spec-enrichment-proposal.json, stub contract, v02.192 spec modules 02/07/10/11/12, spec-changelog.jsonl, SPEC_CURRENT.md, packet.json); attacked criterion coverage AC1-AC4, citation validity, specificity, v02.192 freshness, and scope-artifact existence. Counted 12.7 primitive rows (13, names match exactly) and interaction bullets (5 total incl. the HBR row) directly from the module; verified the addendum framing sentence and the module-12 change plan in the proposal.

items: Corrected interaction-addition count to four + HBR row per adversarial review.

wp_validator_review_v1.review_scope: Validated only MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION in WP-KERNEL-009; no ACP used.

findings.summary: AC1 evidence still overclaims five interaction additions plus the HBR row; the appendix has five bullets total, with the fifth being the HBR row.
findings.required_remediation: Correct the MT-045 evidence wording to match the actual appendix contents. Either add the missing fifth interaction addition in the spec/module evidence, or state that there are four interaction additions plus the HBR row.

evidence_basis: Authoritative MT JSON contract and current product tree inspection.
Dependency failures use deterministic packet status/dependency reads only; WIP MT work products were not inspected.
remediation_summary: Correct the MT-045 evidence wording to match the actual appendix contents. Either add the missing fifth interaction addition in the spec/module evidence, or state that there are four interaction additions plus the HBR row.

remediation_v2.fail_verdict: FAIL_V1 AC1_EVIDENCE_OVERCLAIM (MEDIUM): evidence claimed five interaction additions + HBR row (the fifth 12.7 bullet IS the HBR row)
remediation_v2.remediation: Corrected AC1 wording to four interaction additions plus the HBR row (five 12.7 bullets total). Matches the actual appendix contents.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-045 is a MasterSpecEnrichmentSeed record MT (no implementation_commit, no product code) that registers the WP-KERNEL-009 primitive/interaction family for future spec enrichment. The seed content is real, applied to the appendix, Handshake-native, and consistent with the current Master Spec; the sole prior FAIL_V1 finding (interaction-count overclaim) is verifiably corrected.
findings: MT-045 has no lifecycle.implementation_commit; it is a spec-enrichment-seed/record MT. Spec-Realism sub-rules 1 and 2 are NA per the WP_VALIDATOR_PROTOCOL research/spec/record-MT carve-out; the validation bar is whether the named artifact/spec anchor exists and satisfies the acceptance_criteria.

AC1 satisfied: the seed rows are actually applied in 12-end-of-file-appendices.md#12.7 (current v02.194, lines 13303-13329). Counted directly: 13 primitive additions (PRIM-ProjectKnowledgeIndex ... PRIM-VisualDebugEvidence, lines 13309-13321) and 5 interaction bullets total (lines 13325-13329) = 4 interaction edges + 1 HBR applicability row.

Prior FAIL_V1 (AC1_EVIDENCE_OVERCLAIM, MT-045.json lines 132-141) is FIXED. The remediation_v2 (lines 152-157) corrected the wording; current MT-045 evidence (line 94) reads 'four interaction additions and the HBR applicability row (five 12.7 interaction/HBR bullets total, the fifth being the HBR row, not a sixth)', which matches the appendix exactly.

AC2 satisfied: exact files pinned in scope.relevant_files; supporting artifacts spec-enrichment-proposal.json (module_change_plan references 12-end-of-file-appendices.md at line 110) and refinement.json both exist in the packet dir.

AC3 satisfied: registered rows are Handshake-native primitives, not external references; constraints bar sidecar-paper/outside-runtime proof. Confirmed against current spec 2.3.13.11 (02-system-architecture.md line 2696) which fixes ProjectKnowledgeIndex as a PostgreSQL/EventLedger-backed authority surface explicitly rejecting wiki/markdown-vault/sidecar authority -- consistent with the seed.

AC4 satisfied: HBR-INT-001/HBR-MAN-001/HBR-SWARM-001 named NOT_APPLICABLE for runtime proof on a spec-seed record MT (no product surface/code/test/runtime state changed), and CX-503R PostgreSQL-only law named SATISFIED at spec level via 2.3.13.11; concrete reasons given (line 97).

Sub-rule 3: claimed_by == ready_by == KERNEL_BUILDER, but kernel_builder did NOT sign COMPLETED (status is READY_FOR_VALIDATION_V2; completed_by null). Per protocol, self-cert alone is not a fail and the proof here is real, so no fail.

Aside (non-blocking, expected): contracts pin v02.192 anchors while current spec is v02.194; the same section numbers (12.7, 2.3.13.11) resolve in v02.194, so the stale pin is paperwork drift only, not a code/behavior defect.

Legacy SQLite text remains in 02-system-architecture.md baseline sections (e.g. lines 1158-1283), but those are the pre-existing base-research layer; the WP-009 enrichment 2.3.13.11 overrides them with PG-only authority for this WP's paths, which is exactly what the MT constraint asks the seed to flag as a replacement Handshake-native rule.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
